raw_consumer_statement:
I was charged for XXXX nightstands from XXXX & XXXX, but only one was delivered. This constitutes non-delivery of goods. 

The merchant acknowledged in writing on multiple occasions that a full refund was owed for the undelivered item : On XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX District Manager, XXXX XXXX, confirmed in writing that the refund would be issued via credit card chargeback. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX representative, XXXX XXXX, again confirmed in writing that I was due a full refund of {$590.00}. 

Despite receiving both written admissions, my credit card issuer has refused to permanently credit my account. The case number is Case # XXXX. 

I did not cancel or return merchandise. The second item was never delivered. 

I believe my credit card issuer failed to properly investigate and resolve a billing error involving non-delivery of goods, in violation of the Fair Credit Billing Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1666 ).

I am requesting that the issuer permanently credit my account in the amount of {$590.00} and correct its records.
